back_translation: Samuel took the flask of oil and poured it on his head and kissed him, and said, 'Is it not because the LORD has anointed you over his inheritance as ruler?'

In your going from with me today, you will find two men at the tomb of Rachel in the territory of Benjamin at Zelzah, and they will say to you, 'The donkeys are found which you went to seek, and behold, your father has ceased the matter of the donkeys and is worried for you, saying, What shall I do for my son?'

You shall pass on from there and go further, and you shall come to the oak of Tabor, and three men will find you there going up to God at Bethel — one bearing three young goats, and one bearing three loaves of bread, and one bearing a skin of wine.
They will ask you of peace, and they will give to you two of bread, and you shall take from their hand.

After this you shall come to the hill of God, where there is a garrison of the Philistines, and it shall be at your coming there to the city, you shall meet a band of prophets coming down from the high place — and before them harp and tambourine and flute and lyre — and they shall be prophesying.
The Spirit of the LORD shall rush upon you, and you shall prophesy with them, and you shall be turned into another man.
It shall be when these signs come to you, do for yourself as your hand finds, for God is with you.

You shall go down before me to Gilgal, and behold, I will go down to you to offer up burnt offerings, to sacrifice peace sacrifices. Seven days you shall wait until my coming to you, and I will make known to you what you shall do.
It was as he turned his shoulder to go from with Samuel, that God changed him another heart, and all these signs came on that day.
They came there to the hill, and behold, a band of prophets met him, and the Spirit of God rushed upon him, and he prophesied in their midst.

It was every one who knew him from yesterday and before saw him, and behold, with the prophets he prophesied, and the people, a man said to his brother, 'What is this that has happened to the son of Kish? Is Saul also among the prophets?'
A man from there answered and said, 'And who is their father?' Therefore it became as a proverb, 'Is Saul also among the prophets?'
He finished from prophesying, and came to the high place.
Saul's uncle said to him and to his young man, 'Where did you go?' He said, 'To seek the donkeys, and we saw that there were none, and we came to Samuel.'
Saul's uncle said, 'Tell me now what Samuel said to you.'
Saul said to his uncle, 'Telling he told us that the donkeys were found.' But the matter of the kingship he did not tell him, which Samuel spoke.
Samuel called the people to the LORD at Mizpah.
He said to the sons of Israel, 'Thus says the LORD God of Israel: I brought up Israel from Egypt, and I delivered you from the hand of Egypt and from the hand of all the kingdoms that oppressed you.'

'And you today have rejected your God, who saves you from all your evils and your distresses, and you said to him, A king you shall set over us. And now, stand before the LORD by your tribes and by your thousands.'
Samuel caused all the tribes of Israel to draw near, and the tribe of Benjamin was taken.
He caused the tribe of Benjamin to draw near by its clans, and the clan of Matri was taken, and Saul son of Kish was taken — and they sought him, and he was not found.
They inquired again of the LORD, 'Has the man yet come here?' And the LORD said, 'Behold, he has hidden himself among the baggage.'
They ran and took him from there, and he stood in the midst of the people, and he was taller than all the people from his shoulder and upward.
Samuel said to all the people, 'Do you see whom the LORD has chosen — for there is none like him in all the people?' And all the people shouted and said, 'May the king live!'
Samuel spoke to the people the custom of the kingdom, and wrote in a book and laid up before the LORD, and Samuel sent all the people away, each man to his house.
Saul also went to his house at Gibeah, and there went with him the valiant ones whose hearts God had touched.
But the sons of Belial said, 'How shall this one save us?' And they despised him and did not bring him a gift, and he was as silent.
